description: Lentibulariaceae is the largest family of carnivorous plants, containing three genera: Utricularia (bladderworts, 240+ species worldwide), Pinguicula (butterworts, 100+ species), and Genlisea (corkscrew plants, 30+ species). Utricularia holds the record as the genus with the most carnivorous plant species. Many species are found in India including Utricularia graminifolia, U. striatula, U. purpurascens, and Pinguicula alpina.
meta_title: Lentibulariaceae — Bladderworts, Butterworts & Corkscrew Plants Taxonomy Pack
meta_description: Complete taxonomy of Lentibulariaceae: 240+ Utricularia, 100+ Pinguicula and 30+ Genlisea species with conservation status and cultivation data.

genera.description: The largest genus of carnivorous plants with 240+ species. Found on every continent except Antarctica, in habitats from aquatic to terrestrial to epiphytic. All species trap prey using tiny bladder traps with a trapdoor mechanism. Many produce beautiful flowers disproportionately large to plant size.

Carnivorous plants using sticky mucilaginous leaves to trap small insects and digest them. Found in Europe, Asia, and the Americas. Many Mexican and Central American species are popular in cultivation for their large, colorful flowers.

Unique carnivorous plants that trap microscopic organisms in spiralling underground corkscrew traps. Found in tropical Africa, South America and Madagascar. They have the smallest recorded genome of any plant.
